---
title: "ExploratoryDataAnalysis"
editor: visual
---

```{r}

install.packages("tidycensus")
library(tidycensus)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(sf)
census <- read.csv("~/Desktop/PPA_AVM_Property_Taxes/data/census_dataset_cleaned.csv")
house <- read.csv("~/Desktop/PPA_AVM_Property_Taxes/data/houses_clean.csv")




# Step 1: Pull census block group BOUNDARIES for Philadelphia from tidycensus
census_geo <- get_acs(
  geography = "block group",
  variables = "B19013_001",   # placeholder — you already have your vars
  state = "PA",
  county = "Philadelphia",
  geometry = TRUE             # ← this gives you the polygon shapes
)



#changed GEOID to character
census_geo <- census_geo %>%
  mutate(GEOID = as.character(GEOID))



# Step 2: Join your cleaned census attributes onto those boundaries by GEOID
census_full <- census_geo %>%
  left_join(census, by = "GEOID")



# Step 3: Reproject both to EPSG:3365
census_full <- st_transform(census_full, 3365)
houses_sf   <- st_as_sf(house, wkt = "shape", crs = 2272) %>% 
                st_transform(3365)


# Step 4: Spatial join — each house gets the census attributes of the
#         block group polygon it falls inside
houses_joined <- st_join(houses_sf, census_full, join = st_within)



houses_joined <- houses_joined %>%
  drop_na(MEDHHINCE, Pov_Prop, BACH_PROP, Vacancy_Rate, WHITE_PROP)


st_write(houses_joined, "~/Desktop/PPA_AVM_Property_Taxes/data/census_housing_geo.shp")



```

\
\
\
